skill.name: manifest
skill.description: Install, configure, and verify the Manifest observability plugin for Claude agent telemetry.
skill.summary: Configure Manifest observability for Claude agents with plugin setup, API key entry, endpoint configuration, and log verification.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell-like patterns, network URLs, credential references, and hidden home-directory log access. Review confirms the commands are documented setup steps for the Manifest plugin, but the skill still handles API keys, modifies Claude configuration, installs a plugin, and reads local logs, so publication should include a security warning.

medium_findings.title: Plugin Installation and Gateway Control Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to stop the Claude gateway, install the Manifest plugin, start the gateway, and disable another plugin during troubleshooting. These commands are expected for setup, but they change the local agent runtime and should require user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command lines are direct setup instructions in fenced blocks, so the operational behavior is clear. I did not find evidence of hidden command construction or user-controlled shell injection.

medium_findings.title: API Key Collection and Configuration
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the user for a Manifest API key and writes it into Claude plugin configuration. This is legitimate for the plugin, but it handles a secret and could expose credentials if executed or logged carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly request a key starting with mnfst_ and configure it as apiKey. Safety guidance reduces risk, but secret handling is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: External Manifest Endpoint Configuration
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to the Manifest web service and supports a custom telemetry endpoint. This network behavior matches the skill purpose, but users should verify endpoint ownership before sending telemetry.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and endpoint configuration are visible in the instructions and examples. I did not find evidence that the skill forces an untrusted endpoint without user input.

low_findings.title: Hidden Claude Log Read for Verification
low_findings.description: The skill reads the Claude gateway log under the user home directory to verify plugin startup. The access is read-only and scoped to a log file, but logs can contain sensitive operational details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The log path and grep command are explicit. The risk is limited because the command reads a specific log and filters for manifest entries.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static False Positives
low_findings.description: Several static hits came from Markdown backticks, descriptive text, and key-format examples rather than executable weak cryptography or system reconnaissance. No evidence found of a weak cryptographic algorithm or prompt injection attempt in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines are front matter, routing guidance, setup prose, or API key format text. They are not cryptographic or reconnaissance code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands in Setup Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for Claude gateway and plugin management. They appear intentional and relevant, but they can alter local agent behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly present and match the static findings for external command patterns. They are not obfuscated and are tied to documented installation steps.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential and Telemetry Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines API key handling with endpoint configuration for telemetry. This combination is expected for observability setup but creates exposure risk if misused.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The text directly links API key setup with a default or custom endpoint. The context supports legitimate use, so this is a warning rather than confirmed abuse.

content.value_statement: Agent telemetry setup can be error-prone when keys, plugins, and gateway state are configured manually. This skill gives a structured Manifest setup flow with verification and troubleshooting steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides installation of the Manifest plugin with the Claude CLI.
Collects and validates the expected Manifest API key prefix.
Configures the plugin API key and optional telemetry endpoint.
Restarts or installs the Claude gateway around configuration changes.
Checks gateway logs for Manifest pipeline activation.
Provides fixes for missing keys, invalid keys, unreachable endpoints, and duplicate OTel registration.
limitations: Requires the Claude CLI and gateway commands to be available locally.
Requires a Manifest account and a valid Manifest API key.
Does not design dashboards, alerts, or broader observability architecture.
Custom endpoints must be verified by the user before telemetry is sent.

use_cases.title: First-Time Agent Monitoring Setup
use_cases.description: Install Manifest, enter the API key, and verify that telemetry is active.

use_cases.title: Self-Hosted Endpoint Configuration
use_cases.description: Connect Claude agent telemetry to an approved internal Manifest endpoint.

use_cases.title: Manifest Plugin Troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Check logs and resolve common key, endpoint, and OTel registration issues.

prompt_templates.title: Install Manifest Monitoring
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup with the default Manifest service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to set up Manifest observability for my Claude agent with the default endpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Configure With My API Key
prompt_templates.scenario: User already has a Manifest key ready.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure the Manifest plugin. I have my Manifest API key ready and want you to verify the setup afterward.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Private Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Team uses a self-hosted Manifest instance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Connect my Claude agent to our self-hosted Manifest endpoint and confirm the gateway reports an active pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Manifest Startup
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced support for a failed setup.
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose why Manifest telemetry is not active. Check the gateway status, review relevant logs, and suggest the smallest fix.

output_examples.input: Set up Manifest observability for my Claude agent.
output_examples.output: A guided setup that stops the gateway, installs Manifest, asks for a valid key, configures the plugin, restarts the gateway, and verifies activation.

output_examples.input: Use our internal Manifest endpoint for telemetry.
output_examples.output: A configuration flow that confirms the endpoint, stores it in the plugin settings, and checks the gateway log for a successful connection.

output_examples.input: Manifest is installed but telemetry is not showing up.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist that checks key format, endpoint reachability, gateway logs, and duplicate OTel registration.

best_practices: Confirm the user approves every command that changes plugin or gateway state.
Treat the Manifest API key as a secret and never print it after entry.
Verify custom endpoints before sending telemetry to them.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for general observability design or dashboard planning.
Do not paste Manifest API keys into shared logs, issues, or transcripts.
Do not configure a custom endpoint unless the user confirms it is trusted.

faq.question: What does this skill install?
faq.answer: It guides installation and configuration of the Manifest observability plugin for Claude agents.

faq.question: Does it need a Manifest API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The setup requires a Manifest key that starts with the expected mnfst_ prefix.

faq.question: Can it use a self-hosted Manifest endpoint?
faq.answer: Yes. It can configure a custom endpoint when the user confirms the endpoint is trusted.

faq.question: Does it expose my API key?
faq.answer: The skill says not to log or echo the key, but the key is stored in local Claude plugin configuration.

faq.question: Can it fix duplicate OTel registration?
faq.answer: It includes a troubleshooting step that disables a conflicting diagnostics OTel plugin when needed.

faq.question: Is this a dashboard builder?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on plugin setup, endpoint configuration, log verification, and basic troubleshooting.
